standards.name: Next.js App Router
risks: Framework choice should still follow product/runtime constraints.
useWhen: Default web app and website implementation target for Dhiraj projects.
avoidWhen: A tiny static prototype has no routing/data needs, or a client requires another stack.

standards.name: React
risks: Agent-generated code can overuse client state without clear ownership.
useWhen: Component-driven web UI and agent-generated reusable components.
avoidWhen: The target is a non-React codebase.

standards.name: TypeScript
risks: Overly broad types can create false confidence.
useWhen: Every reusable component API and agent-generated interface.
avoidWhen: Throwaway prototypes that will never be reused.

standards.name: Tailwind CSS
risks: Utility sprawl without tokens can make Dhiraj identity inconsistent.
useWhen: Default styling system for speed, tokens, and component portability.
avoidWhen: A project mandates CSS modules/design-system package only.

standards.name: Radix/shadcn-style Primitives
risks: Default shadcn look can feel generic without signature tokens and composition.
useWhen: Accessible dialogs, sheets, tabs, tooltips, popovers, and form surfaces.
avoidWhen: Purely static marketing sections with no interactive primitive need.

standards.name: Performance Budgets (CWV)
risks: Animation bloat can tank INP and LCP scores.
useWhen: Establishing thresholds for JS bundle size, LCP (<2.5s), and INP (<200ms).
avoidWhen: Never.

standards.name: 2025 Standard Breakpoints
risks: Relying on old breakpoints (like 768px only) misses the modern mobile-large/foldable tier.
useWhen: Defining layout tiers: 480px (Mobile), 768px (Tablet), 1024px (Laptop), 1280px (Desktop), 1536px (Ultra-Wide).
avoidWhen: Purely fluid 'intrinsic' layouts where no media queries are used.

standards.name: Sidebar / App Shell Nav
risks: Poor mobile transition; must switch to Bottom Tab Bar or Drawer on small screens.
useWhen: Complex SaaS products and admin dashboards with 6+ primary sections.
avoidWhen: Simple marketing sites (use Top Nav).

standards.name: Skeleton Loading (Shimmer)
risks: Static skeletons can feel 'stuck' if data loading is extremely slow.
useWhen: Preventing layout shift during async data fetching (Suspense).
avoidWhen: Instant-load static content.

standards.name: WCAG 2.2 Compliance
risks: Accessibility is binary; non-compliance is a legal and usability failure.
useWhen: Every project, specifically focusing on focus-visible, keyboard nav, and ARIA landmarks.
avoidWhen: Never.

standards.name: Fluid Typography Scale
risks: Viewport units (vw) can scale poorly in sidebars/nested containers; prefer cqi.
useWhen: Responsive layouts requiring robust, mathematical type scaling.
avoidWhen: Fixed-width layouts where clamp() is unnecessary.

standards.name: SaaS Information Density
risks: High density can overwhelm casual users; requires 'Opinionated Design'.
useWhen: Pro-tools, dashboards, and data-heavy SaaS products (Linear/Stripe style).
avoidWhen: Consumer landing pages or 'hand-holding' onboarding flows.

standards.name: Command Palette (Cmd+K)
risks: Hidden feature problem; needs visible affordance/shortcuts list.
useWhen: High-performance pro-tools and complex dashboards.
avoidWhen: Simple marketing sites with 3-5 pages.

standards.name: Dark/Light Theme Persistence
risks: Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC) on page load.
useWhen: Cookie-based persistence for pure RSC/App Router; next-themes for simple abstraction.
avoidWhen: Raw localStorage without a blocking script.

standards.name: Performance Font Loading
risks: Layout shift (CLS) and extra DNS lookups.
useWhen: next/font for automatic self-hosting, size-adjust, and variable font support.
avoidWhen: External @import or <link> for fonts.

standards.name: Tremor Raw
risks: Higher initial code volume compared to a library.
useWhen: Production dashboards requiring full control over charts and KPI cards.
avoidWhen: Simple charts where a generic library is sufficient.

standards.name: Page / View Transitions
risks: Experimental status, network-dependent jank, and lack of exit control.
useWhen: Shared Element Transitions (e.g., card-to-hero) in Next.js 15.
avoidWhen: Mission-critical production apps requiring precise exit-animation control.

standards.name: Composition Over Configuration
risks: Can lead to code duplication if not carefully managed.
useWhen: Building production-ready components that need to be customized beyond simple props.
avoidWhen: Atomic components where props are sufficient.

standards.name: GSAP + ScrollTrigger
risks: Imperative animation cleanup in React, bundle cost, and temptation to animate too much.
useWhen: ScrollTrigger work, SVG/path motion, complex timelines, creative websites, advanced text reveals.
avoidWhen: Simple UI state transitions where CSS or Motion is enough.

standards.name: Better-Auth
risks: Relative newcomer compared to Clerk/Auth.js but highly extensible.
useWhen: Open-source, self-hosted or serverless native auth for 2025.
avoidWhen: Enterprise mandates specific SSO/managed-only auth.

standards.name: Drizzle ORM
risks: Requires more SQL knowledge than Prisma.
useWhen: Performance-first, serverless-native data access.
avoidWhen: Team is already expert and invested in Prisma or raw SQL.

standards.name: Lenis
risks: Scroll feel can hurt accessibility, browser expectations, and mobile performance.
useWhen: Creative/marketing pages where scroll is a major narrative surface.
avoidWhen: Dashboards, forms, data grids, native app-like workflows, or reduced-motion users.

standards.name: Three.js + React Three Fiber
risks: Asset pipeline, GPU performance, mobile fallbacks, and low-level rendering complexity.
useWhen: Memorable 3D product scenes, portfolio/brand microsites, WebGL interactions.
avoidWhen: Routine web apps, content sites, or where a static/video asset communicates better.

standards.name: React Hook Form + Zod
risks: Schemas drift if form and API validation are duplicated carelessly.
useWhen: All non-trivial forms, settings, onboarding, signup, and checkout flows.
avoidWhen: Single uncontrolled search inputs or static newsletter mocks.

standards.name: Figma
risks: Static mockups miss motion and coded states unless design engineering closes the gap.
useWhen: Visual exploration, tokens, flows, and critique before coding.
avoidWhen: Coded prototype is faster for highly interactive motion.

standards.name: Canonical Base Component Set
risks: Too many variants can create contradictions; keep one canonical interaction per component.
useWhen: Every Dhiraj web app/site starter.
avoidWhen: Never avoid; prune only for tiny prototypes.

standards.name: Navigation/Header
risks: Over-designed nav can hide core product navigation.
useWhen: All apps/sites as the primary orientation surface.
avoidWhen: Fullscreen immersive microsites may delay nav until after the first scene.

standards.name: Hero Section
risks: Generic oversized SaaS hero can feel template-like.
useWhen: Landing pages, product intros, portfolios, app onboarding pages.
avoidWhen: Dense operational dashboards where users need task context first.

standards.name: Bento/Grid Feature System
risks: Bento layouts become noise if every tile competes for attention.
useWhen: Feature overviews, product capabilities, portfolio proof, dashboard summaries.
avoidWhen: Plain forms or task flows where a linear layout is clearer.

standards.name: Accessible Form System
risks: Errors and async states are often under-designed.
useWhen: Auth, onboarding, settings, checkout, contact, and workflow inputs.
avoidWhen: Static display-only cards.

standards.name: Dialog/Sheet
risks: Focus traps and mobile sizing fail when hand-rolled.
useWhen: Confirmations, settings panels, creation flows, mobile side panels.
avoidWhen: Long multi-step workflows that deserve their own route.

standards.name: Toast/Feedback
risks: Toast-only errors can be inaccessible or easy to miss.
useWhen: Non-blocking success, undo, save, sync, and background feedback.
avoidWhen: Critical errors that need inline or modal recovery.

standards.name: Tooltip/Popover
risks: Critical information can become inaccessible if hidden only in hover content.
useWhen: Icon labels, lightweight contextual help, and small supplemental details.
avoidWhen: Core instructions, errors, or content required to complete a task.

standards.name: Loading/Empty/Error States
risks: Blank states make otherwise polished products feel unfinished.
useWhen: Every data-driven component and async workflow.
avoidWhen: Never avoid; use lightweight forms where needed.

standards.name: Footer
risks: Footer can become a dumping ground for unprioritized navigation.
useWhen: Secondary navigation, legal links, contact, social proof, and persistent site metadata.
avoidWhen: Immersive single-scene microsites where footer would break the concept.

standards.name: Dhiraj Header Text Reveal
risks: Text splitting can harm accessibility and resize behavior if implemented carelessly.
useWhen: Short hero headlines, section titles, major route entrances.
avoidWhen: Long paragraphs, legal text, dense dashboards.

standards.name: Scroll Reveal
risks: Overuse makes pages feel repetitive; content must remain reachable without JS.
useWhen: Section entrance, progressive disclosure, visual hierarchy.
avoidWhen: Critical content that must appear immediately.

standards.name: Sticky/Pinned Sections
risks: Mobile viewport quirks and scroll confusion.
useWhen: Narrative feature sections, side-by-side product explanations, stacked cards.
avoidWhen: Task-heavy apps, forms, content with unpredictable height.

standards.name: Card Hover Depth
risks: Hover-only affordances fail on touch.
useWhen: Cards, bento tiles, clickable previews.
avoidWhen: Mobile-only critical affordance.

standards.name: Modal/Sheet Transition
risks: Slow modals reduce perceived responsiveness.
useWhen: Dialog/sheet open and close states.
avoidWhen: Users prefer reduced motion.

standards.name: Dhiraj Smooth Scroll
risks: Smooth scroll can become scroll hijacking if applied to product workflows.
useWhen: Creative/marketing pages where scroll feel is part of the brand experience.
avoidWhen: Dashboards, forms, data grids, dialogs, and reduced-motion users.

standards.name: Reduced Motion Support
risks: Skipping this is an accessibility failure.
useWhen: Every animated component.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: State Completeness
risks: Polished still frames hide broken product behavior.
useWhen: Every component spec and implementation.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Motion Tokens
risks: Inconsistent timing makes a system feel stitched together.
useWhen: Every Dhiraj motion spec.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Choose DOM, SVG, Canvas, or WebGL Deliberately
risks: Wrong surface causes performance, maintainability, or accessibility failures.
useWhen: Every non-trivial animation decision.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Real Product UX Over Award-Site Theater
risks: Award patterns can damage real usability when copied without context.
useWhen: Every final component and page decision.
avoidWhen: Never avoid.

standards.name: Dhiraj Signature Layer
risks: Signature choices must not override accessibility or product clarity.
useWhen: Hero copy, section rhythm, scroll feel, toast behavior, and motion defaults.
avoidWhen: Client brand systems that require a different visual language.

standards.name: Competence Feedback
risks: Can become manipulative if used as empty gamification.
useWhen: Learning, fitness, creation, habit, onboarding, and progress-based products.
avoidWhen: Decorative points, badges, and leaderboards with no real user goal.

standards.name: SVG Motion
risks: Many animated SVG nodes can hurt performance and screen-reader clarity.
useWhen: Logo, icon, path drawing, morphing, masks, data-viz accents.
avoidWhen: Bulk UI animation better handled by DOM/CSS.

standards.name: Cinematic GLSL Shaders
risks: Specialist skill, GPU cost, accessibility fallback, and mobile performance.
useWhen: Brand-defining scenes, premium product moments, interactive portfolio work, adding cinematic textures.
avoidWhen: Default app surfaces or utility workflows.

standards.name: 3D-to-2D Morphing
risks: Extremely high implementation complexity.
useWhen: Seamlessly transitioning from immersive 3D intro to functional UI.
avoidWhen: Budget or timeline constraints prevent high-end custom GLSL work.

standards.name: Depth Map Parallax
risks: Requires high-quality AI depth maps and Three.js plane warping.
useWhen: Adding 3D-like depth to static hero images without 3D models.
avoidWhen: Standard 2D parallax is sufficient.

standards.name: Animation Everywhere
risks: AI-slop feel, cognitive load, slow perceived performance, weak trust.
useWhen: Never.
avoidWhen: Always.

standards.name: Heavy Scroll Hijacking
risks: Broken browser expectations, mobile jank, accessibility problems.
useWhen: Never as a base default.
avoidWhen: Product workflows, forms, dashboards, content-heavy pages, reduced-motion users.

standards.label: WCAG 2.2 Compliance for Animated UI
standards.description: All animated components must respect prefers-reduced-motion, provide keyboard navigation, maintain focus management, and preserve semantic HTML for screen readers. Text splitting must use aria-label on parent and aria-hidden on decorative spans.
standards.rationale: WCAG 2.3.3 explicitly requires user control over non-essential animation. Text splitting accessibility is frequently overlooked but critical.

standards.label: Evidence-Based Typography System
standards.description: Use fluid typography with clamp() and rem units for accessibility. Heading scale via Utopia.fyi or equivalent. Body at 16-18px base. Font stack: Inter/Satoshi for UI, optional serif for editorial headings. Variable fonts preferred. High-contrast pairing (grotesk + serif) for brand character.
standards.rationale: Every award-winning site uses intentional typography. The corpus lacked any font/type-scale tokens. Kevin Powell + Utopia.fyi + Awwwards font trends all converge.

standards.label: Animation Performance Budget
standards.description: Total animation JS budget: <80KB gzipped. GSAP core (~23KB) + Motion (~2.3-46KB) + Lenis (~3KB) = ~50-72KB. Use modular GSAP imports. Lazy-load heavy animation components with next/dynamic { ssr: false }. Never animate LCP element with JS. Stick to transform/opacity for GPU compositing. Avoid layout-triggering properties. Target: LCP <2.5s, INP <200ms, CLS <0.1.
standards.rationale: Performance budget directly impacts CWV, SEO, and user retention. Animation libraries can easily exceed 100KB without discipline.

standards.label: Product/SaaS Design Principles (Linear Standard)
standards.description: Product UI prioritizes clarity/speed/logic over decoration. Consistent 8px spacing scale. Bold typography + high contrast. Keyboard-first UX with shortcuts. Modular component system. Goal-oriented dashboards for specific audiences. Actionable insights with contextual clarity. Boring but better > flashy but fragile.
standards.rationale: The corpus was 90% creative/portfolio. Linear/Vercel/Stripe represent the product-side standard that must inform dashboard and SaaS templates.

standards.label: Fluid-First Responsive Design
standards.description: Minimize breakpoints; use clamp(), min(), max() for fluid scaling. Container queries for component-level responsiveness. Fluid type scales maintain rhythm across viewports. Touch targets minimum 48px. Mobile navigation: consider tab bar, drawer, or collapsible sidebar over hamburger alone.
standards.rationale: Modern CSS eliminates the need for many media queries. Kevin Powell + web.dev consensus on fluid-first approach.

standards.label: System-Aware Dark/Light Theme
standards.description: Detect system preference via prefers-color-scheme. Use next-themes for SSR-safe switching with suppressHydrationWarning. CSS custom properties toggle light/dark palettes. Persist user choice in cookie (SSR) or localStorage. Both themes must pass WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text).
standards.rationale: Every major component ecosystem supports dark mode. The existing design-tokens.css already has .dark values but lacked the implementation standard.

standards.label: View Transitions API for Page Navigation
standards.description: Use the native View Transitions API with Next.js experimental.viewTransition. Wrap layouts with React ViewTransition component. Use view-transition-name CSS for shared element morphing. Progressive enhancement: graceful fallback to instant navigation. GPU-compositor performance. Prefer over JS-based page transition libraries.
standards.rationale: Widespread browser support in 2025-2026. Official Next.js integration. GPU-native performance far exceeds JS alternatives. Still experimental in Next.js.

standards.label: Font Loading Performance Strategy
standards.description: Use next/font for automatic self-hosting and optimization. font-display: swap for body text, optional for decorative display fonts. Prefer variable fonts (single file, multiple weights). Self-hosted > CDN for privacy and LCP. Subset fonts to used character ranges when possible.
standards.rationale: Font loading directly impacts LCP. next/font is the standard in Next.js. Variable fonts reduce HTTP requests while enabling fluid weight.

standards.label: Marquee / Logo Ticker
standards.description: Infinitely scrolling horizontal strip for logos, testimonials, or text. CSS animation preferred with requestAnimationFrame fallback. Pause on hover. Reduced-motion: static grid or single row.
standards.rationale: Present in Magic UI, Aceternity UI, Animate UI, Hover.dev. Common in SaaS landing pages and portfolio sites.

standards.label: Command Palette / Search (⌘K)
standards.description: Global search/action modal triggered by keyboard shortcut. Fuzzy search, categorized results, recent items. Use Radix Dialog + custom search logic. Focus trap, keyboard navigation, escape to close.
standards.rationale: Linear, Vercel, Raycast, and Notion all feature command palettes. Becoming a product UI standard for power users.

standards.label: Skeleton Loading (Shimmer)
standards.description: Placeholder shapes matching expected content layout during data fetching. Shimmer gradient animation via CSS. Matches final content dimensions to prevent CLS. Reduced-motion: static gray blocks without shimmer.
standards.rationale: Industry standard loading pattern. Prevents CLS. Used by every major SaaS product.
